meaning: adv.喋喋不休地,口若悬河地
examples: In the next booth he could see an elderly lady, talking volubly.
"She’s got a way with her, has Susan," she went on quite volubly.
He talked as volubly as ever.
Nestled into a banquette one Sunday night at 44, a fashionable Manhattan restaurant, he is talking volubly when another diner approaches.
But, most volubly, the left will try to win the argument that free-trade deals are dangerous and should either be amended or rejected.
